subject: Accounting
title: Return on Equity
description: Learn Return on Equity in one minute.

hook.text: You invest $100,000 in a business. It earns $15,000. Is that good? ROE tells you.

buildup.text: Return on equity divides net income by shareholders' equity.

discovery.text: A 15% ROE means the company generates $0.15 of profit for every dollar of equity.

twist.text: High ROE can be misleading if it's driven by massive debt rather than genuine efficiency.

climax.text: Buffett looks for consistently high ROE—it signals a durable competitive advantage.

punchline.text: ROE measures how hard your money works for you.

quiz.question: How is return on equity calculated?
options: Net income divided by shareholders' equity
